items.briefing:
Scamwatch is warning Australians about a surge in scams impersonating the ATO and myGov. These messages typically pressure people to act urgently about tax debts or account issues — if you receive an unexpected call, text, or email claiming to be from either service, do not click links or provide personal details. Separately, a new report highlights that cyberattackers are increasingly targeting education software suppliers rather than schools directly, which is relevant for registered training organisations that rely on third-party EdTech platforms.

The Australian Communications and Media Authority has also signalled stronger consumer protection as a compliance focus for 2026–27, meaning organisations handling personal data should ensure their practices are up to date. **Practical tip:** If you receive any unexpected ATO or myGov communication, go directly to the official website by typing the address yourself rather than following any link provided.

featured.title: Australia's resilience problem isn't redundancy. It's shared infrastructure
featured.quote: Shared infrastructure vulnerabilities could cause widespread Australian outages despite redundancy measures.
